sources.name: Federal Highway Administration — National Scenic Byways

paragraphs: The Mississippi River Parkway Planning Commission was formed in 1938 to develop plans for what was to become the Great River Road. Secretary of the Interior Harold Ickes urged the governors of the 10 states along the Mississippi River to form the commission. State planning officials had been developing the concept of a Mississippi River Parkway as an extension of an idea for a recreational river road that had been first put forward by the Missouri Planning Board in 1936.

The commission was instrumental not only in the early planning and development of the parkway but also in its construction, promotion, marketing, and development. (Now known as the Mississippi River Parkway Commission and headquartered in Minneapolis, the commission continues to promote, preserve, and enhance the resources of the Mississippi River Valley and the Great River Road. Representatives of the 10 states and two Canadian provinces serve on the commission's board of directors, and they serve as chairpersons of their state Mississippi River Parkway commissions.)[citation needed]

The U.S. House of Representatives' Committee on Public Lands held hearings in 1939 and 1940 to discuss a bill that would have authorized a feasibility study of the Mississippi River Parkway concept. While popular, the parkway idea was soon overshadowed by World War II.

It wasn't until 1949 that Congress approved funding for a feasibility study. The study, "Parkway for the Mississippi River", was completed by the Bureau of Public Roads (BPR) (predecessor agency to the Federal Highway Administration) in 1951.

When the Great River Road meanders inland, it offers vistas of fields and farmsteads. Barns in Wisconsin are noteworthy, their designs having evolved over time. Location: Location: (44.588° N 92.396° W) Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: Car Passing a Lake on the in Arkansas
gallery.desc: A car passes the blue waters of a lake on the Great River Road on an autumn day. Location: Location: Arkansas (33.596° N 91.219° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Dennis Adams of FHWA
Flooded trees may line the riverbanks beside the Great River Road. Location: Location: Arkansas (35.697° N 89.957° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Dennis Adams of FHWA
The sun sets dramatically over the Mississippi River along Mississippi's Great River Road. Location: Location: Mississippi (32.315° N 90.907° W) Status: Public domain.

The Wisconsin Great River Road meanders away from the river at times to provide variety in scenery such as the forested hills and valleys of Pierce County. Location: Location: Wisconsin (44.579° N 92.378° W) Status: Public domain.
